notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR The following fundamentals course: THEA 50: Fundamentals of Theater Production One course from the following series: THEA 61A: Ancient and Medieval Drama THEA 61B: Drama from the Renaissance to the Modern Age THEA 61C: The Birth of the Modern: Drama and Performance After the Renaissance Plus one course from the student's area of focus chosen from the following: THEA 10: Introduction to Theater Design and Technology THEA 20: Introductory Studies in Acting THEA 21: Acting Studio I: Psychological Realism THEA 30: Introduction to Dance Theory and Technique THEA 40: Introduction to Directing Please visit the Performance, Play & Design website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
